channel.title: Psalms on Christian Internet Resources
channel.description: Recent content in Psalms on Christian Internet Resources

item.title: Psalms 1
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 1

BOOK 1

1 People are blessed that don’t

                        take advice from the wicked

                        or stand among sinners

                        or sit with scoffers,

                           2but delight in the Law of the LORD

                        and meditate on it day and night.

            3 They’re like trees planted beside a river

item.title: Psalms 2
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 2

1 Why do the nations get together

      to plot pointless schemes?

2 Kings take their stand

      and rulers hold counsel

            against the LORD and his Anointed One [Messiah]

                  [Acts 4:25-26]:

         3“Let’s break their bonds

item.title: Psalms 3
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 3

A Psalm of David When He Fled from His Son Absalom

1 LORD, I have so many enemies;

      so many are working against me;

         2so many are saying,

            “God’s not going to help him.”                                  Ps 3:1-2

3 But you’re my shield, LORD, my glory,

item.title: Psalms 4
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 4

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

                                                            with Stringed Instruments

1 Righteous God, answer when I call!

         Take time for me in my distress;

      listen graciously to my prayer.

2 People, how long will you try to ruin my reputation?

         How long will you love unfounded accusations and outright lies?

item.title: Psalms 5
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 5

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

                                                             with Flute Accompaniment

1 Listen to me, LORD;

         Pay attention to my groaning.

2 Hear my call for help, my God and King,

      because I pray only to you.

3 You hear me in the mornings,

item.title: Psalms 6
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 6

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

                                                            with Stringed Instruments;

                                          Set to The Sheminith [To the Eight-String Lyre]

1 Don’t lash out at me, LORD,

         or take me to task in your anger.

2 Be gracious to me because I’m weak;

item.title: Psalms 7
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 7

                                                A Shiggaion of David that He Sang

to the LORD About Cush, a Benjamite

1 LORD my God, I’ve taken refuge in you;

         protect me from persecutors

2 so they won’t tear me apart like a lion does

      and drag me away with no one to rescue me.

item.title: Psalms 8
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 8

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

                                                                  Set to The Gittith

1 LORD, our Lord, you’re so majestic all over the world!

You display your splendor higher than the sky!

2 Out of the mouth of infants and nursing babies [Mt 21:16; see LXX]

      you’ve established a defensive wall

item.title: Psalms 9
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 9

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

Set to the tune of “Death of the Son”

1 I’ll thank you, LORD, with all my heart

      and call attention to the amazing things you’ve done.

2 I’ll gladly celebrate over you

      and sing your praise, Most High.

item.title: Psalms 10
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 10

1 Why stand so far away, LORD?

         Why hide when trouble comes?

2 Sinners hunt down the poor in arrogance

      and get themselves caught in their own schemes.

3 They brag about their sinful desires;

         they bless the greedy and curse the LORD.

item.title: Psalms 11
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 11

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

1 I rely on the LORD’s protection;

            so why tell me,

      “Flee like a bird to the mountains

         2because the ungodly are bending the bow;

            they’re putting their arrow to the string

item.title: Psalms 12
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 12

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

Set to The Eight-String Lyre

1 Help, LORD; there aren’t any decent people left;

      the faithful are disappearing.

2 Neighbors lie to one another

      and speak with flattering lips and deceitful hearts.

3 May the LORD cut off their flattering lips

item.title: Psalms 13
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 13

1 LORD, are you going to forget about me forever?

      How long will you ignore me?

2 How long must I counsel myself in constant sorrow?

      How long will my enemies lord it over me?

3 Think about me and respond to me, LORD my God;

item.title: Psalms 14
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 14

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

1 Fools keep telling themselves there’s no God,

      corrupt people that do corrupt things;

      none of them does what’s right.

2 The LORD looks down from heaven

         to see if there’s anybody that lives wisely,

item.title: Psalms 15
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 15

A Psalm of David

1 LORD, who can live in your Tabernacle?

      Who can reside on your holy hill?

2 Those who act with integrity,

            who do what’s right and tell the truth;

                  3who don’t mistreat their neighbors

                  or join in gossiping about their friends;

item.title: Psalms 16
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 16

A Mikhtam of David

1 Protect me, LORD; you’re my refuge.

2 I say, “You’re my Lord;

      apart from you, I don’t have anything good.”

3 The godly people on earth are the noble ones I delight in.

4 Trouble multiplies

                  for those who rush off to some other god;

item.title: Psalms 17
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 17

A Prayer of David

1 LORD; listen to my call for justice;

      pay attention to my sincere prayer.

2 Declare me innocent

         because you see that I’m righteous.

3 You’ve visited me at night and tested my heart;

      you’ve put me on trial and found nothing wrong;

item.title: Psalms 18
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 18

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David,

the LORD’s servant, who sang this song to him

when he delivered him from his enemies and from Saul

He said,

1 “I love you, LORD, my strength,

2 my fortress, my deliverer,

item.title: Psalms 19
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 19

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David.

1 The heavens show God’s glory;

      the sky displays his workmanship.

2 Day in and day out they speak;

      night after night they display intelligence.

3 There’s no speech or words;

      there’s nothing to hear.

4 Their testimony goes out to the whole world,

item.title: Psalms 20
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 20

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

1 When trouble comes, may the LORD answer you!

      May Jacob’s God set you safely on high!

2 May he send help from the sanctuary

      and support you from Zion!

3 May he remember your many meal offerings

item.title: Psalms 21
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 21

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

1 LORD, the king delights in your strength;

         he’s happy because you’ve helped him.

2 You’ve given him his heart’s desire;

      you didn’t withhold his request.                                      

3 You met him with blessings

      and crowned him with fine gold.

item.title: Psalms 22
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 22

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

Set to The Hind of the Dawn

1 My God, my God, why have you forsaken me? [Mt 27:46; Mk 15:34]

         Why are you so far from helping me,

            so far from my groaning?

2 I call out in the daytime, but you don’t answer;

item.title: Psalms 23
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 23

A Psalm of David

1 The LORD is my shepherd;

      I’ll have everything I need.

2 He beds me down in green pasture

      and leads me beside still water.

3 He refreshes me

      and guides me along good paths for his honor.

4 Even though I walk through the darkest valley,

item.title: Psalms 24
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 24

A Psalm of David

1 The earth and everything in it belongs to the LORD,

      the world and those who live here [1 Cor 10:26].

2 He positioned land higher than seas,

      more elevated than ocean currents.

3 Who can climb his mountain

item.title: Psalms 25
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 25

A Psalm of David

1 I hold my life up to you, LORD;

2 I trust you;

         don’t let me down

      or let my enemies celebrate over my defeat.

3 Nobody that waits for you will be discredited;

      people who deal treacherously

item.title: Psalms 26
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 26

A Psalm of David

1 Declare me innocent, LORD,

         because I’ve conducted myself with integrity

      and trusted in you without wavering.

2 Examine me, put me on trial,

         test my motives and attitude.

3 I keep my eye on your unfailing love

item.title: Psalms 27
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 27

A Psalm of David

1 The LORD is my light and salvation;

            who am I going to be afraid of?

      He defends me;

            who am I going to dread?

2 When bad people come to devour me,

      they stumble and fall.

item.title: Psalms 28
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 28

A Psalm of David

1 I’m calling out to you, LORD;

         don’t turn a deaf ear, my Rock;

      otherwise, I’ll be like someone going down to the Pit.

2 Help me when I plead for help,

      when I lift up my hands toward your holy sanctuary.

item.title: Psalms 29
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 29

A Psalm of David

1 Children of the mighty,

      attribute to the LORD honor and strength.

2 Honor him like he deserves;

      worship him in his holy splendor.

Ps 29:1-2

3 The sound from the LORD carries out over the water;

item.title: Psalms 30
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 30

A Psalm of David

                                                A Song at the Dedication of the Temple

1 I’ll praise you, LORD, because you’ve rescued me;

      you haven’t let my enemies celebrate victory over me.

2 I appealed to you for help, my God,

                  and you restored my health;

item.title: Psalms 31
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 31

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

1 I take refuge in you, LORD;

      never let me be disgraced;

      in your goodness protect me.

2 Listen to me, rescue me quickly;

      be a rock for me,

      a strong fortress to save me.

item.title: Psalms 32
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 32

A Maskil. A Psalm of David

1 How blessed they are whose sin God forgives,

      whose transgression he covers;

         2whose disobedience the LORD doesn’t hold against them,

      whose spirit doesn’t harbor deceit! [Rom 4:7-8]

3 When I didn’t acknowledge my sin,

item.title: Psalms 33
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 33

1 Sing for joy to the LORD, good people;

      praise is fitting for the godly.

2 Give him thanks with a lyre;

      sing praises to him with a ten-string harp.

3 Sing a new song to him;

      play skillfully and shout for joy.

4 You can believe what he says

item.title: Psalms 34
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 34

A Psalm of David When He Faked Insanity

                                          in front of Abimelech, Who Drove Him Away

1 I’ll bless the LORD at all times

      and praise him constantly.

2 I’ll make my boast about him;

      the helpless will hear it and take heart.

item.title: Psalms 35
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 35

A Psalm of David

1 LORD, oppose the ones that oppose me;

         fight those who fight me.

2 Put on buckler and shield,

      and come to my aid.

3 Draw spear and javelin against my pursuers;

      tell me you’ll save me.

item.title: Psalms 36
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 36

For the Choir Director

                                                A Psalm of David, the LORD’s Servant

1 Sin speaks to sinful people’s hearts;

         they don’t respect God at all [Rom 3:18].

2 They flatter themselves

         that he can’t discover their sin.

item.title: Psalms 37
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 37

A Psalm of David

1 Don’t worry about bad people

            or envy those who do wrong;

         2they’ll wither like grass

            and fade like flowers.

3 Trust in the LORD and do good

      so you can live in the land and enjoy security.

item.title: Psalms 38
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 38

A Psalm of David

for a Memorial

1 LORD, don’t rebuke me in your wrath

      or discipline me in your furious anger.

2 Your arrows have pierced deep into me;

      your hand has come down hard on me.

3 Because of your indignation, my body’s not sound;

item.title: Psalms 39
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 39

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

Set to Jeduthun

1 I said, “I’ll watch what I do

            so I won’t sin with my tongue;

      I’ll put a muzzle on my mouth

            when sinful people come around.”

2 But when I kept silent even about what was good,

item.title: Psalms 40
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 40

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

1 I waited patiently for the LORD;

      he listened to my call for help.

2 He brought me up out of the pit of despair,

            out of the muddy clay,

      set my feet on solid rock,

item.title: Psalms 41
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 41

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

1 How blessed people are that have regard for the poor;

      the LORD will deliver them when they have need.

2 He’ll protect them, keep them alive, and call them blessed;

         3sustain them when they’re sick and restore them to health.

item.title: Psalms 42
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 42

BOOK 2

                                                            For the Choir Director

A Maskil of the Sons of Korah

1 My soul pants for God

         like a deer pants for water.

2 My soul is thirsty for the living God;

      when will I ever see him?

3 My tears have been my food day and night

item.title: Psalms 43
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 43

1 Declare me innocent, God;

         defend me against this ungodly nation;

      deliver me from liars!

2 You’re the God that I rely on for courage;

                  why reject me?

Why do I go around in mourning

      because my enemies oppress me?

3 Send your light and truth to lead me,

item.title: Psalms 44
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 44

                                                            For the Choir Director

A Maskil of the Sons of Korah

1 God, our ancestors have told us

      what you did in their time long ago.

2 You drove out nations and planted this one;

         you struck them and scattered them abroad.

3 Our ancestors didn’t conquer the land with their sword;

item.title: Psalms 45
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 45

                                                      For the Choir Director. Set to Lilies

                                          A Maskil of the Sons of Korah. A Song About Love

1 My heart is overflowing with a noble theme

      as I recite my verses to the king;

      my tongue is like the pen of a gifted poet.

2 You’re more handsome than the sons of men;

item.title: Psalms 46
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 46

                                                            For the Choir Director

A Psalm of the Sons of Korah

Set to Alamoth. A Song

1 God is our refuge and strength,

      an ever-present help when trouble comes.

2 So we won’t be afraid

                  though the ground tremors,

            though the mountains cascade into the sea,

item.title: Psalms 47
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 47

                                                            For the Choir Director

                                                      A Psalm of the Sons of Korah

1 Clap your hands, everybody;

            shout to God with joy;

         2because you are to revere the LORD Most High,

            the great King over the whole world.

3 He subdued people groups under us,

item.title: Psalms 48
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 48

                                                A Song. A Psalm of the Sons of Korah

1 The LORD is great and deserves great praise

      in the city of our God on his holy mountain.

2 Mount Zion, the heights of Zaphon, is high and beautiful—

         the joy of the whole earth, the city of the great King.

item.title: Psalms 49
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 49

                                                            For the Choir Director

                                                      A Psalm of the Sons of Korah

1 Hear this, every nation;

      listen, everyone on earth,

         2low and high, rich and poor alike.

3 My words are wise;

         my thoughts are full of insight.

4 I listen to proverbs

item.title: Psalms 50
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 50

A Psalm of Asaph

1 God, the Mighty One, the LORD, has spoken

      and calls to earth from sunrise to sunset.

2 He shines forth out of Zion in perfect beauty.

3 Our God comes; he doesn’t stay quiet;

      a fire consumes in front of him;

item.title: Psalms 51
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 51

                                          Psalm for the Choir Director. A Psalm of David,

When Nathan the Prophet Came to Him,

After He’d Slept with Bathsheba

1 Be gracious to me, God, in proportion to your enduring love;

      according to your great mercy, blot out my transgressions.

2 Wash me thoroughly from my sin;

item.title: Psalms 52
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 52

For the Choir Director. A Maskil of David,

                                           When Doeg the Edomite Came and Told Saul,

“David has gone to Ahimelech’s house.”

1 God’s enduring love lasts all day;

         so why brag about sinning, great soldier?

2 Your tongue cuts like a razor.

      3 You like evil more than good,

item.title: Psalms 53
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 53

                                                For the Choir Director. Set to Mahalath

A Maskil of David

1 Fools tell themselves there’s no God.

They’re corrupt and commit disgusting sins;

      none of them does what’s good.

2God looks down from heaven

      to see if anybody understands,

      if anybody looks to him.

item.title: Psalms 54
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 54

For the Choir Director. A Maskil of David,

                                                When the Ziphites Came and Told Saul,

“Isn’t David hiding among us?”

On Stringed Instruments

1 Deliver me, God, by your name;

      vindicate me by your power.

2 Listen to my prayer; hear what I have to say.

item.title: Psalms 55
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 55

                                                For the Choir Director. A Maskil of David

                                                            On Stringed Instruments

1 Hear my prayer, God;

      don’t ignore my plea.

2 Listen and answer;

         trouble has overwhelmed me; I’m distracted

                  3by the threats of my enemies,

            by bringing suffering on me

item.title: Psalms 56
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 56

For the Choir Director. A Mikhtam of David,

When the Philistines Seized Him in Gath

Set to The Pigeon in Far-Off Terebinths

1 Be gracious, God; people are walking all over me,

      oppressing me all the time, 2attacking me.

3 When I’m afraid, I put my trust in you.

item.title: Psalms 57
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 57

                                          For the Choir Director. A Mikhtam of David,

                                                When He Fled from Saul in the Cave

                                                             Set to Do Not Destroy

1 Be gracious to me, God,

      because I depend on you to protect me;

      in the shadow of your wings I take refuge

            till danger passes.

item.title: Psalms 58
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 58

                                                For the Choir Director. A Mikhtam of David

Set to Do Not Destroy

1 Leaders, do you decree what’s right?

         Do you judge justly?

2 No, you plot injustice in your hearts

         and spread violence in the land.

                        3 The sinful go astray from the womb;

item.title: Psalms 59
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 59

                                          For the Choir Director. A Mikhtam of David,

                                    When Saul Sent Men to Watch the House and Kill Him

                                                            Set to Do Not Destroy

1 Deliver me from my enemies, God;

      set me on high away from my attackers.

2 Save me from sinners;

      rescue me from bloodthirsty people.

item.title: Psalms 60
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 60

                                          For the Choir Director. A Mikhtam of David,

                          to teach when he struggled with Aram-naharaim and Aram-zobah,

                        and Joab came back and struck 12,000 Edomites in the Valley of Salt

1 You’ve rejected us, God, and broken us;

      you’ve been angry, but restore us now.

2 You’ve made the land quake; you’ve split it open;

item.title: Psalms 61
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 61

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

                                                            On Stringed Instruments

1 Listen to my prayer, God; hear my prayer.

2 With a faint heart I call to you from the end of the earth;

         lead me to the rock that’s higher than me.

3 You’re my refuge, my strong tower against the enemy.

item.title: Psalms 62
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 62

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

Set to Jeduthun

1 I wait for God alone

      because my victory comes from him.

2 He alone is my rock, my salvation, my fortress;

I won’t be shaken.

3 How long will youpl assail me

      so you can murder me,

item.title: Psalms 63
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 63

A Psalm of David,

When He Was in the Wilderness of Judah

1 God, you’re my God; I look to you in earnest;

      my soul thirsts for you;

      my flesh yearns for you in this dry, weary land.

2 So I’ve looked at you in the sanctuary

      and seen your power and glory.

item.title: Psalms 64
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 64

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

1 Listen to my complaint, God;

      keep me from living in dread of my enemies.

2 Hide me from the secret plots of bad people,

      from the schemes of sinners

                  3that sharpen their tongue like a sword,

            that aim bitter words like arrows,

item.title: Psalms 65
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 65

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

A Song

5 You answer us with awesome deeds, God who keeps us safe,

      who is trusted in every land and the farthest sea.

6 You established the mountains by your power

                  and dressed yourself with strength,

         7you that still the roaring seas

item.title: Psalms 66
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 66

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm. A Song

1 Make a joyful sound to God, all the earth.

2 Sing about his honor;

      make his praise glorious.

3 Say to him, “What you do is so awesome!

      Your enemies feign obedience to you

            out of fear for your power.

item.title: Psalms 67
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 67

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm. A Song

With Stringed Instruments

1 God, be gracious and bless us;

            smile at us

         2so every nation can know your way                               

                  and your saving power.

3 May the nations praise you;

      may they all praise you.

item.title: Psalms 68
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 68

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

A Song

1 Arise, God, scatter your enemies;

      chase off those who hate you!

2 Blow them away like smoke;

         melt them like wax near fire;

      make sinners perish in your presence.

3 But may good people be happy,

item.title: Psalms 69
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 69

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

Set to Lilies

1 Save me, God;

      I’m in water up to my neck!

2 I’ve sunk in deep mud, where there’s no foothold;

      a flood is sweeping over me.

3 I’m worn out from crying; my throat’s dry;

item.title: Psalms 70
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 70

For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

                                                                   for a Memorial

1 Hurry and deliver me, LORD;

      come quick to help me.

2 Discredit those who want to kill me;

      turn them back and

      dishonor the people that delight in harming me.

item.title: Psalms 71
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 71

1 I’ve taken refuge in you, LORD;

      don’t let me be discredited.

2 In your goodness, rescue me;

      listen to me and save me.

3 Be my rock of refuge that I can always go to;

      give orders to save me

            because you’re my rock and fortress.

item.title: Psalms 72
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 72

A Psalm of Solomon 

1 Give the king your judgments, God;

      give your goodness to his son.

2 May he judge your people fairly,

      your oppressed ones with justice.

3 Let the mountains bring prosperity to the people;

      may the hills bring goodness.

4 May he defend the downtrodden,

item.title: Psalms 73
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 73

BOOK 3

A Psalm of Asaph

1 God is good to Israel,

         to those whose hearts are pure.

2 I’d almost stumbled; my steps almost slipped;

         3because I envied the prosperity of the arrogant.

4 They don’t seem to suffer when they die;

      their bodies are healthy and well.

item.title: Psalms 74
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 74

A Maskil of Asaph

1 Why have you thrown us away forever, God?

      Why does your anger smoke at the sheep in your pasture?

2 Remember your community that you bought so long ago,

      that you redeemed as a tribe for your heritage,

      Mount Zion, where you’ve lived.

3 Direct your attention to its continuing ruins;

item.title: Psalms 75
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 75

For the Choir Director. A Psalm of Asaph

A Song; Set to Do Not Destroy

1 Thank you, God, for being close by;

      people declare your wonderful deeds.

2 “When I set a time for judgment;

      I’ll judge even-handedly.

3 The earth and everybody in it melt;

item.title: Psalms 76
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 76

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of Asaph

                                                      A Song. On Stringed Instruments

1 God is known in Judah;

         his name is great in Israel.

2 His home is in Salem;

         he lives on Zion.

3 He has broken flaming arrows there,

item.title: Psalms 77
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 77

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of Asaph

Set to Jeduthun

1 I raise my voice up to God; I call out loud;

      it rises to him so he’ll hear me.

2 When trouble comes, I turn to the LORD for help.

      All night I reach out to him,

item.title: Psalms 78
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 78

Maskil of Asaph

1 Listen to my instruction, my people;

      pay attention to what I’m saying.

2 I’ll tell a parable

         and offer hidden truths from our history [Mt 13:35],

      3 things we’ve heard and known from our ancestors.

item.title: Psalms 79
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 79

A Psalm of Asaph

1 Other nations have invaded your inheritance, God;

      they’ve defiled your holy Temple

      and laid Jerusalem in ruins.

2 They’ve given the buzzards your servants’ corpses,

      and the wild animals the flesh of your holy people.

3 They’ve poured out their blood like water around Jerusalem;

item.title: Psalms 80
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 80

For the Choir Director. A Psalm of Asaph

Set to Lilies; A Testimony

1 Listen, Shepherd of Israel,

            you that lead Joseph like a flock;

      show your glory,

            you that are enthroned above the cherubs.

2 Stir up your power against Ephraim, Benjamin, and Manasseh;

item.title: Psalms 81
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 81

For the Choir Director. A Psalm of Asaph

Set to The Gittith

1 Sing to God your Strength;

      make a joyful sound to Jacob’s God.

2 Sing, beat the tambourine,

      pluck the lyre and harp.

3 At the new moons blow your ram’s horn,

      at the full moon and on your festival days.

item.title: Psalms 82
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 82

A Psalm of Asaph

1 God presides over the divine council

      and judges you judges.

2So how long will youpl judge unjustly

      by showingpl partiality to lawbreakers?                           

3 Renderpl justice to the weak and fatherless;

      standpl up for the oppressed and destitute.

4 Rescuepl the weak and needy;

item.title: Psalms 83
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 83

A Song. A Psalm of Asaph

1 God, don’t keep quiet; don’t be silent.

2 Your enemies are making an uproar;

      those who hate you are exalting themselves.

3 They’re plotting against your people,

         conspiring against them 4 and saying,

item.title: Psalms 84
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 84

                                    For the Choir Director. A Psalm of the Sons of Korah

Set to The Gittith

1 Your Temple is so beautiful, LORD of Hosts!

         2I long, yes, even faint with longing for your courts;

      my body and soul sing for joy to the living God.

item.title: Psalms 85
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 85

For the Choir Director. A Psalm of the Sons of Korah

1 LORD, you blessed your land;

            you restored the fortunes of Jacob;

         2you forgave your people’s iniquity

            and pardoned their sins;

         3you withdrew your fury

            and turned away from your hot anger.

item.title: Psalms 86
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 86

A Prayer of David

1 Listen, LORD, and answer me

      because I’m troubled and in need.

2 Preserve my life because I’m a godly man;

      save me because I trust in you.

3 Be gracious to me

      because I call out to you all the time.

item.title: Psalms 87
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 87

                                                A Song. A Psalm of the Sons of Korah

1 The city that the LORD founded stands on his holy mountain;

         2he loves Zion more than the other places in Jacob.

3 Glorious things are said about you, city of God.                 

4 “I’ll count Rahab [Egypt] and Babylon among those who know me,

item.title: Psalms 88
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 88

                                          For the Choir Director. A Psalm of the Sons of Korah.

                                                      A Song. Set to Mahalath Leannoth

                                                       A Maskil of Herman the Ezrahite

1 LORD God who keeps me safe,

      I call out to you day and night.

2 Let my prayer come to you;

item.title: Psalms 89
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 89

A Maskil of Ethan the Ezrahite

1 I’ll sing forever about the LORD’s enduring love

      and make known his faithfulness to every generation,

2 because I’ve said, “You build up enduring love;

      in the heavens you establish your faithfulness.”

 3The LORD said, “I’ve made a covenant with my chosen one;

item.title: Psalms 90
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 90

BOOK 4

A Prayer of Moses, the Man of God

1 LORD, through all generations

         you’ve been our dwelling place.

2 Before the mountains were born

         or you gave birth to the world,

      from one end of time to the other, you’ve been God.

item.title: Psalms 91
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 91

1 Those who live in the shelter of the Most High

      will find rest in the shadow of the Almighty.

2 They’ll say about the LORD,

      “Our refuge and fortress, the God we trust!”                 

Ps 91:1-2

3 He delivers you from the trapper’s snare

            and deadly pestilence.

item.title: Psalms 92
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 92

A Psalm

A Song for the Sabbath

1 It’s good to thank you, LORD,

      to sing praises to you, Most High;

      2 to declare your enduring love in the morning

            and your faithfulness at night

            3 with a lute and a ten-stringed harp,

item.title: Psalms 93
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 93

1 The LORD reigns, robed in majesty,

            dressed in strength.

      The world stands firm; it won’t move.

2 Your throne has been secure from of old;

      you’re from eternity past.

3 The floods have swelled;

         the roar of their pounding waves has increased.

item.title: Psalms 94
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 94

1 LORD God of vengeance, shine!

2 Rise up, Judge of the whole world;

      repay the proud.

3 How long will you let sinners celebrate?

4 How long will they pour out their arrogant words,

      vaunt themselves up

         5crush your people,

      attack your heritage

item.title: Psalms 95
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 95

1 Come, let’s sing to the LORD;

      let’s make a joyful sound to the Rock that saves us.

2 Let’s go to him with thanksgiving

      and sing to him with psalms.

3 The LORD is a great God,

      a great King above all gods.

item.title: Psalms 96
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 96

1 Sing a new song to the LORD;

      sing to him, all the earth.

2 Bless him in song;

      proclaim every day the good news that he saves.

3 Tell about his glory the world over,

      his marvelous deeds among the nations.

4 The LORD is great and deserves great praise;

item.title: Psalms 97
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 97

1 The LORD reigns; let the world rejoice;

      let the distant shores be glad.

2 Clouds and dense darkness surround him;

      goodness and justice are the foundation for his throne.

3 Fire goes forth ahead of him

      and burns up his adversaries.

4 His lightning lights up the world;

item.title: Psalms 98
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 98

A Psalm

1 Sing a new song to the LORD

            because he’s done outstanding things;

      his right hand and holy arm have given him victory.

2 He has made his salvation known

      and revealed his goodness to the nations.

3 He has remembered his enduring love

item.title: Psalms 99
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 99

1 The LORD reigns;

                  let the people tremble;

      he sits on his throne above the cherubs;

            let the earth quake!

2 The LORD is great in Zion;

      he’s exalted above all nations.

3 Let them praise his great, awesome name;

item.title: Psalms 100
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 100

A Psalm for Thanksgiving

1 Make a joyful sound to the LORD, all the earth.

2 Serve him gladly;

         come to him with singing.

3 Acknowledge that the LORD is God;

      he made us; we didn’t make ourselves.

item.title: Psalms 101
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 101

A Psalm of David

1 I’ll sing about your enduring love and justice,

      and sing praises to you, LORD.

2 I’ll pay attention to the way that’s blameless;

      when will you come to help me?

      In my home I’ll walk in integrity.

3 I won’t set anything base beside me;

item.title: Psalms 102
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 102

                                                A Prayer by the Oppressed When They’re Faint

and Pour Out Their Complaint to the LORD

1 Listen to my prayer, LORD!

      Let my cry come to you.

2 Don’t turn away from me in my distress;

         when I call, answer quickly.

item.title: Psalms 103
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 103

A Psalm of David

1 Bless the LORD, all that I am!

      Everything that’s in me, bless him.

2 Bless the LORD, all that I am;

      May I never forget how he helps me:

                  3he pardons my sins

item.title: Psalms 104
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 104

1 Bless the LORD, everything that I am!

      LORD my God, you’re so great;

      you’re dressed in splendor and majesty.

2 You cover yourself with light as with a robe;

      you’ve stretched out the sky like a tent.

3 You laid the beams for your rooms on the water;

item.title: Psalms 105
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 105

1 Be grateful to the LORD; call on him;

      tell the nations what all he’s done.

2 Sing praises to him;

      talk about the amazing acts.

3 Take pride in his holy name;

      let the people be happy that rely on him.

4 Rely on the LORD and his strength;

item.title: Psalms 106
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 106

                                                                        1Hallelujah!

Be grateful to the LORD because he’s good;

      his enduring love lasts forever.

2 Who can recount all his mighty deeds?

      Who can ever praise him enough?

3 How blessed they are who maintain justice,

      who practice goodness all the time!

4 Remember me, LORD, when you’re gracious to your people;

item.title: Psalms 107
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 107

BOOK 5

1 Be grateful to the LORD because he’s good;

      his love endures forever.

2 Let his redeemed say it,

      the ones he rescued from their adversaries

      3 and gathered from other countries

      east and west, north and south.

item.title: Psalms 108
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 108

A Song. A Psalm of David

1 I’m steadfast, God;

      I’ll sing praises in my heart.

2 Wake up, harp and lyre;

      I’ll wake up at dawn!

3 I’ll thank you, LORD, among the nations

            and sing praises to you everywhere,

item.title: Psalms 109
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 109

For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

1 God that I praise, speak up for me!

2 Deceitful people speak against me;

         they slander me with lies.

3 They surround me with hateful words

      and fight me for no reason.

4 In return for my love, they accuse me

item.title: Psalms 110
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 110

A Psalm of David

1 The LORD said to my Lord,

      “Sit at my right hand

            till I make your enemies your footstool”

                  [Mt 22:44; Mk 12:36; 14:62; Lk 20:42; 22:69; Acts 2:34; Heb 1:13].

2 The LORD will extend your mighty scepter from on Zion,

item.title: Psalms 111
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 111

                                                                        1Hallelujah!

I thank the LORD with all my heart

      in the presence of his godly people.

2 His works are so outstanding

            no one that delights in them

            can quit thinking about them;

         3their splendor and majesty

            reflect his eternal goodness;

item.title: Psalms 112
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 112

                                                                        1Hallelujah!

How blessed people are that respect the LORD

      and delight in what he calls on them to do.

2 Their descendants will be successful

      and a blessing to their own generation.

3 They’ll have wealth and riches in their homes,

      and their goodness will last forever.

item.title: Psalms 113
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 113

                                                                        1Hallelujah!

Servants of the LORD, praise him;

         2bless him from now on forever;

         3from sunrise to sunset, praise him.

4 On high above the nations,

      he displays his glory in the sky.

5 Who is like the LORD our God

item.title: Psalms 114
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 114

1 When Israel left Egypt—

      when the House of Jacob came out

            from a nation with a different language—

2 the land of Judah became his sanctuary;

      Israel became his kingdom.

3 The Red Sea saw them coming and backed away;

      the Jordan quit flowing.

item.title: Psalms 115
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 115

1 Not to us, LORD, not to us,

      but to you be honor

      because of your enduring love and faithfulness.

2 Why should the nations say,

      “Where’s their God?”

3 Our God is in heaven,

      where he accomplishes what he intends.

4 Their man-made idols are just silver and gold

item.title: Psalms 116
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 116

1 I love the LORD because he listens to my petitions;

         2so I’ll call on him as long as I live.

3 The snares of death were all around me;

      the pains of Sheol came on me;

      I suffered distress and sorrow.

4 Then I called on the LORD,

item.title: Psalms 117
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 117

1 Praise the LORD, every nation;

         praise him, every people group [Rom 15:11],

2 because he has great enduring love toward us;

      hisY faithfulness lasts forever.

                                                Hallelujah!                            

Ps 117:1-2

item.title: Psalms 118
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 118

1 Be grateful to the LORD because he’s good;

                        his love lasts forever.

2 Let Israel say,

                        “His love lasts forever.”

3 Let the house of Aaron say,

                        “His love lasts forever.”

4 Let those who respect the LORD say,

item.title: Psalms 119
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 119

Aleph א

1How blessed they are who follow his way blamelessly,

      who follow the Law of the LORD,

         2who observe his testimonies

      and look to him with all their heart.

3 They do no evil who follow his ways.

item.title: Psalms 120
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 120

A Song of Ascents

1 In my troubles I appealed to the LORD,

      and he answered me.

2 LORD, rescue me from lying lips

      and treacherous tongues.

3 What will God do to you, treacherous tongues?

      How will he intensify your punishment?

item.title: Psalms 121
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 121

A Song of Ascents

1 I look up toward the mountains—

      will my help come from there?

2 It’ll come from the LORD,

      who made earth and sky.

3 He won’t let your foot slip.

The One that guards you won’t slumber;

item.title: Psalms 122
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 122

                                                       A Song of Ascents. by David

1 It made me happy when they said,

      “Let’s go to the LORD’s Temple.”

2 We’re standing inside your gates, Jerusalem,

                  3that’s built solid and seamless,

         4where the tribes of the LORD go up as required by the Law

item.title: Psalms 123
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 123

A Song of Ascents

1 I look to you enthroned in heaven!

2 Like a servant looks to his master

      and a maid looks to her mistress,

      we look to the LORD our God

            to show us mercy.

3 Be gracious to us, LORD,

item.title: Psalms 124
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 124

A Song of Ascents. by David

1 Let Israel acknowledge,

         “If the LORD hadn’t taken our side

         2when people attacked us,

                  3they would have swallowed us alive in their fury,

                  4the water would have overwhelmed us,

item.title: Psalms 125
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 125

A Song of Ascents

1 Those who trust in the LORD are like Mount Zion,

      that can’t be moved, that lasts forever.

2 Like mountains surround Jerusalem,

         the LORD surrounds his people

      from now on forever.

3 Sinners won’t rule

item.title: Psalms 126
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 126

A Song of Ascents

1 When the LORD brought us exiles back to Zion,

      we were like people dreaming.

2 Our mouths were laughing,

            our tongues were shouting with joy;

      the other nations were saying of us,

            “The LORD has done great things for them.”

item.title: Psalms 127
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 127

                                                      A Song of Ascents, by Solomon

1 Unless the LORD builds the house,

            they work for nothing who build it;

      unless the LORD guards the city,

            its watchmen stay awake for no reason.

2 In vain you rise early and work late

item.title: Psalms 128
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 128

A Song of Ascents

1 How blessed you are that respect the LORD

      and follow his ways.

2 When you eat what you earn,

      you’ll be happy, and life will be good.

3 Your wife will be like a fruitful vine in your house;

      your children will be like olive plants around your table.

item.title: Psalms 129
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 129

A Song of Ascents

1 Let Israel say,

         2“They’ve persecuted me often since I was young,

            but they haven’t defeated me.

         3My back looks like a farmer plowed it

            and made long furrows.”

4 The LORD is good;

item.title: Psalms 130
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 130

A Song of Ascents

1 LORD, I’ve cried out to you from the depths;

         2be attentive to my petitions.

3 If you kept track of sins, who could stand?

         4But you forgive so people can continue to respect you.

5 I wait for the LORD

item.title: Psalms 131
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 131

                                                      A Song of Ascents, by David

1 LORD, my heart’s not proud or my eyes arrogant;

      I don’t insert myself into things I can’t handle.

2 I’ve calmed and quieted myself;

      like a weaned child resting against its mother,

      my soul is quiet within me.

item.title: Psalms 132
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 132

A Song of Ascents

1 LORD, remember David and what he suffered;

      2 how he swore to the LORD

      and vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob:

3 “I won’t go to my own house

                  or get on my bed 4 and sleep

item.title: Psalms 133
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 133

A Song of Ascents. by David

1 It’s so good and pleasant

      for brothers to live together in peace.

2 It’s like precious anointing oil

         running down like it did on Aaron’s beard

      and dripped on his robe.

3 It’s like the dew from Mount Hermon

item.title: Psalms 134
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 134

A Song of Ascents

1 Bless the LORD, you servants of the LORD

      that serve him at night in his Temple!

2 Raise your hands toward the sanctuary

      and bless him.

3 May the One that made earth and sky

         bless you from Zion.

item.title: Psalms 135
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 135

                                                                        1Hallelujah!

Praise the LORD;

      praise him, his servant,

         2you that stand in his Temple,

         in the courts of our God!

3 Praise him because he’s good;

      sing his praises because he’s gracious,

                  4because he’s chosen Jacob for himself,

item.title: Psalms 136
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 136

1 Give thanks to the LORD because he’s good.

His love is everlasting.

2 Give thanks to the God of gods.

His love is everlasting.

3 Give thanks to the Lord of lords,

His love is everlasting.

      4 to him alone that does great things,

His love is everlasting.

         5to him that made the sky in his wisdom,

item.title: Psalms 137
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 137

1 We sat by the rivers in Babylon

      and cried when we remembered Zion.

2 We hung our harps on its willows.

3 Our captors required us to sing there;

      our tormentors demanded happy songs,

      “Sing us a song from Zion.”                                           

Ps 137:1-3

4 How can we sing about the LORD in a foreign land?

item.title: Psalms 138
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 138

A Psalm of David

1 I thank you with all my heart

      and sing your praises in front of the mighty.

2 I bow toward your holy Temple

         and thank you for your enduring love and faithfulness,

      because you’ve guaranteed your promises

            with the honor your name has.

item.title: Psalms 139
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 139

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

1 LORD, you’ve known me and examined me.

2 You know when I sit down and when I get up;

      from a long way off you know what I’m thinking.

3 You search out my traveling and my resting;

      you’re well acquainted with my ways.

item.title: Psalms 140
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 140

                                                For the Choir Director. A Psalm of David

1 LORD, rescue me from sinful people

      protect me from violent men,

                  2that plot evil things in their minds,

            and constantly stir up conflict.

3 They have pointed tongues like snakes;

      vipers’ venom lies behind their lips [Rom 3:13b].

item.title: Psalms 141
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 141

A Psalm of David

1 LORD, I’m calling on you; come quick;

      hear me out.

2 Consider my prayer as incense

      and the raising of my hands as an evening offering.

3 Guard my mouth;

      watch over my lips.

4 Incline my heart away from anything evil,

item.title: Psalms 142
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 142

                                                A Maskil of David when he was in the cave

A Prayer

1 I cry out to the LORD

            and petition him;

         2I pour out my complaint to him

            and tell him my troubles.

3 When I feel overwhelmed,

item.title: Psalms 143
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 143

A Psalm of David

1 Hear my prayer, LORD;

      listen to my petition;

      in your faithfulness and goodness, come to my relief.

2 Don’t put me on trial

      because, compared to you, nobody’s good.

3 My enemy has chased me,

      crushed me on the ground,

item.title: Psalms 144
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 144

A Psalm of David

1 Bless the LORD, my Rock,

      who trains my hands for war,

            my fingers for battle.

2 He’s my enduring love and fortress,

      my stronghold and deliverer,

      my shield and refuge,

            who subdues nations.

item.title: Psalms 145
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 145

                                                      A Psalm of Praise. by David

1 I’ll praise you highly, my God and king,

      and bless you forever.

2 I’ll bless you every day

         and praise you from now on.

3 You’re great and deserve great praise;

      you have a greatness we have no way to measure.

item.title: Psalms 146
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 146

1 Praise the LORD, my soul!

2 I’ll praise my God as long as I live

      and sing praises to him as long as I’m here.

3 Don’t rely on rulers,

      mortals, who can’t help.

4 They quit breathing and go back into the ground;

      their plans come to an end at the same time.

item.title: Psalms 147
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 147

                                                                        1Hallelujah!

      It’s good to sing praises to our God;

      praising him is fitting because he’s gracious.

2 The LORD builds up Jerusalem

      and gathers the outcasts in Israel.

3 He heals the brokenhearted

      and bandages up their wounds.

4 He counts the stars

item.title: Psalms 148
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 148

                                                                        1Hallelujah!

         Praise the LORD from heaven;

      praise him in the sky.

2 Praise him, all his hosts of angels.

3 Praise him, sun and moon

      and shining stars.

4 Praise him, highest heaven,

      and water vapor in the sky.

item.title: Psalms 149
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 149

                                                                        1Hallelujah!

Sing the LORD a new song;

      praise him in the assembly of his holy people.

2 Be happy in your Maker, Israel;

      celebrate your King, people of Zion.

3 Praise him with dancing;

            sing praises with tambourines and lyres,

         4because the LORD delights in you

item.title: Psalms 150
item.description:
Psalms Chapter 150

                                                                        1Hallelujah!

      Praise God in his sanctuary;

      praise him in his heavenly expanse.

2 Praise him for his powerful deeds

      in proportion to his greatness.

3 Praise him with trumpet, harp, and lyre.

4 Praise him with tambourines and dancing,

      with stringed instruments and flutes.
